name: Alain Senderen's Salade De Fruit Exotique (Hh)
recipeInstructions:
1. This ideal fruit salad or dessert is perfect for winter, as tropical fruits are available year-round and ripen naturally. The riper and more fragrant the fruit, the better the salad.
2. In a small saucepan, combine 1 1/2 cups water with the sugar, vanilla bean, coriander seeds, clove, five-spice powder, lime zest, lemon zest, ginger, and mint (if using). Simmer over moderate heat for 10 minutes.
3. Set aside to cool. Stir in the fresh lime juice. The syrup can be made ahead of time and refrigerated for up to a week before serving the salad.
4. Slice off the top and rind from the pineapple; quarter it lengthwise. Reserve 3 quarters for another use. Slice 1 quarter crosswise into 1/8-inch thick pieces.
5. Peel the mango and slice lengthwise, parallel to the pit on both sides, into disks about 1/8-inch thick. Slice each mango disk in half lengthwise.
6. Peel the kiwis and halve them lengthwise. Cut each half into thin wedges.
7. Divide the fruit evenly among 4 shallow soup bowls, overlapping slices to form a decorative pattern.
8. Strain the reserved syrup and divide equally among the 4 servings. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 1 hour or up to 4 hours.
9. Just before serving, halve the passion fruits crosswise. Using a teaspoon, scoop out the pulp from each half and add it to the fruit in each bowl.
